summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases: ZC-2023-007 (major modification to the Woodhull Crossing PUD, formerly the Reserves at Barclay Woods) and ZC-2023-008 (Cedar Grove Estates final development plan). The commission heard staff presentation and developer testimony for the Woodhull Crossing plan proposing 225 single-family homes with updated lot dimensions, improved traffic circulation, reduced cul-de-sacs, and added amenities. Neighboring property owners raised significant concerns about buffering from adjacent industrial uses, drainage issues, traffic safety at the State Route 48 entrance, and the change from a full entrance to an emergency-only access on Charles Snyder Road. The commission voted to approve the Woodhull Crossing modification with staff recommendations and a traffic study condition.
decisions: Motion to approve ZC-2023-007 (Woodhull Crossing PUD major modification) with staff recommendations and requirement for an updated traffic study - passed
action_items: Developer (Robert Gerwin/Coach Development) to provide updated traffic study for State Route 48 entrance
Developer to work with certified arborist/landscape architect to prepare tree retention and landscape plan
Developer to provide landscape plan addressing screening/buffering between PUD and adjacent industrial properties including mounding and pine trees
Developer to provide site plan details for proposed amenities (pickleball courts, dog park, playground, picnic area, walking trails)
Developer to provide building elevations, construction materials, and floor plans as part of final development plan
Developer to address 20-foot buffer encroachment on three lots near primary access road
Developer to address stormwater drainage concerns raised by adjacent property owners (Cornwell property pipe/drainage issue)
Staff to ensure notification letters are sent with adequate lead time and clearer language (per Carl's feedback about acronyms)
key_amounts: 2.86 units per acre density (maximum allowed is 3 units per acre)
30.7% open space (up from 23% in previous plan, but 4.3% below minimum requirement)
Average lot size of 7,625 square feet (minimum required is 5,000 sq ft)
Lot dimensions changed from 70x116 feet to 61x125 feet
Minimum 1,400 square feet floor area required per home
Ranch homes typically 1,600 to 2,000 square feet
notable_topics: Woodhull Crossing PUD major modification (formerly Reserves at Barclay Woods Phase 2)
Lot dimension changes to accommodate ranch home construction
Buffering and screening between residential PUD and adjacent industrial properties
Traffic safety concerns at State Route 48 entrance
Change from full entrance to emergency-only access on Charles Snyder Road
Stormwater drainage management and impact on adjacent properties
Community amenities: pickleball courts, dog park, playground, picnic area, walking trails
Industrial noise, lighting, and odor impacts from adjacent tank truck building operation
Cedar Grove Estates final development plan (second case mentioned but not detailed in transcript)
attendees: Carl (owner of industrial property at 6468 Charles Snyder Road)
Carl Pringle (owner at 6444 Charles Snyder Road)
notes: The transcript contains significant portions of inaudible or garbled text (represented as '...' sequences), making it difficult to capture all details of the second zoning case (ZC-2023-008 Cedar Grove Estates) and the full deliberation/voting details. The PUD was originally approved in 2018 with no subsequent development activity. Developer Robert Gerwin has 22 years of development experience in Clermont County. Adjacent industrial property owner (tank truck builder) expressed strong concerns about future noise complaints. The original 2018 plan had two full entrances; the current plan has one main entrance on SR 48 and an emergency-only access on Charles Snyder Road. Reference was made to previous zoning commission member Elvin Miller recommending a minimum 10-foot dirt mound with 8-foot trees for buffering.
title: ZC 12-6-23
